scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the live REST API reference (buildkite.com/docs/apis/rest-api and its ~20 linked resource pages, fetched 2026-07-04): 6 read streams (organizations, pipelines, builds, agents, teams, clusters) and 17 write actions covering pipeline/build/job/agent/team lifecycle mutations. Excluded: build-scoped and job-scoped annotations/artifacts list+delete endpoints as an ENGINE_GAP (the engine's fan_out dialect resolves exactly one parent id list per stream, but these resources need TWO levels of path nesting resolved together — organization to pipeline to build — which no single fan_out declaration can express; create_annotation itself IS modeled since it needs no read-side id discovery, just a pipeline_slug/build_number pair supplied by the caller), team-membership and team-pipeline/team-suite access-grant join resources, cluster/queue/agent-token/pipeline-template/rule/portal infrastructure-configuration and credential-shaped surfaces, organization audit-log and membership lists, and non-data self-identity/meta endpoints (access-token introspection, current user, API meta) as out_of_scope/requires_elevated_scope/non_data_endpoint breadth-vs-cost triage for this pass; single-object detail endpoints duplicating an already-covered list stream, and binary artifact-download, round out the exclusion set.

excluded.reason: single-organization detail; identical record shape to the organizations list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-pipeline detail; identical record shape to the pipelines list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
regenerates the pipeline's provider (GitHub/GitLab/etc) webhook URL/secret; a one-time provisioning action, not a syncable data mutation

cross-organization all-builds list (every org the token can see); this connector is single-organization-scoped (config.organization), and the org-scoped builds stream already covers every build within it

organization-wide (cross-pipeline) builds list; the pipeline-scoped builds stream already covers the identical per-build record shape, and this connector has no per-pipeline fan-out config to merge results back with a pipeline_slug the org-wide endpoint's response already embeds — kept out to avoid a second stream with fully overlapping record content
single-build detail; identical record shape to the builds list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
changes a queued job's dispatch priority; a narrow scheduling-tuning action, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, no consuming write modeled this pass
returns the job's raw console log text (large unstructured blob), not a syncable structured record
irreversibly deletes a job's console log output; not exposed as a write action this pass pending destructive-scope review
returns the job's resolved environment variables (may include secrets injected by the pipeline); sensitive and not modeled as ordinary sync data

requires a two-level fan-out (enumerate pipelines, then per-pipeline builds, then per-build annotations) that the engine's fan_out dialect cannot express — fan_out resolves exactly one parent id list per stream (bundle.go's FanOutSpec), and this resource needs both a pipeline slug AND a build number threaded into the same path simultaneously; ENGINE_GAP, not a scoping choice

deletes a specific annotation by uuid; no read stream enumerates annotation uuids (see the excluded list-annotations entry above), so there is no way to obtain a valid uuid to delete without an external source

job-scoped alternate path for the same annotations resource; same ENGINE_GAP reasoning as the build-scoped list (a two-level org->pipeline->build fan-out would still be needed to discover which builds/jobs to enumerate)

job-scoped alternate path for create_annotation; the build-scoped write action already covers annotation creation with an equivalent body, and a caller with only a job_id in hand (not a pipeline_slug/build_number pair) is an uncommon shape for this connector's config surface
job-scoped alternate path for deleting an annotation; same reasoning as the build-scoped delete above (no read stream enumerates annotation uuids)
same two-level fan-out ENGINE_GAP as build-scoped annotations (org->pipeline->build path nesting the fan_out dialect cannot express in one stream)
job-scoped alternate path for the same artifacts resource; same two-level fan-out ENGINE_GAP
single-artifact detail; no read stream enumerates artifact ids (see the excluded list-artifacts entries above) to obtain one from
downloads the raw artifact file content (arbitrary binary), not JSON record data
deletes an artifact by id; no read stream enumerates artifact ids to obtain one from
single-agent detail; identical record shape to the agents list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-team detail; identical record shape to the teams list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
team-membership list; a distinct join-resource sub-list, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, no consuming stream this pass
single team-membership detail; part of the team-membership surface excluded this pass
adds a user to a team; part of the team-membership surface excluded this pass
changes a team member's role; part of the team-membership surface excluded this pass
removes a user from a team; part of the team-membership surface excluded this pass
team-to-pipeline access-grant list; a join-resource sub-list, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single team-pipeline access-grant detail; part of the excluded join-resource surface
grants a team access to a pipeline; part of the excluded join-resource surface
changes a team's pipeline access level; part of the excluded join-resource surface
revokes a team's pipeline access; part of the excluded join-resource surface
team-to-test-suite access-grant list (Buildkite Test Engine); a join-resource sub-list, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single team-suite access-grant detail; part of the excluded join-resource surface
grants a team access to a test suite; part of the excluded join-resource surface
changes a team's test-suite access level; part of the excluded join-resource surface
revokes a team's test-suite access; part of the excluded join-resource surface
single-cluster detail; identical record shape to the clusters list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
creates a new cluster; low-frequency infrastructure-provisioning action, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, no consuming write modeled this pass
updates a cluster's name/description/default-queue; low-frequency infrastructure-config action, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
permanently deletes a cluster and detaches every pipeline/queue/agent-token associated with it; not exposed as a write action this pass pending destructive-scope review
cluster-scoped agent-queue list; infrastructure-configuration sub-resource, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, no consuming stream this pass
single-queue detail; part of the excluded queues surface
creates an agent queue; part of the excluded queues surface
updates an agent queue; part of the excluded queues surface
deletes an agent queue; part of the excluded queues surface
pauses job dispatch on a queue; part of the excluded queues surface
resumes job dispatch on a queue; part of the excluded queues surface
lists agent registration tokens; these are bearer credentials agents use to connect, never modeled as ordinary sync data
single agent-token detail; credential-shaped, same reasoning as the list
mints a new agent registration token; a credential-minting operation, never a data mutation
renames/revokes-and-reissues an agent token; credential-shaped mutation
revokes an agent registration token; credential-shaped mutation, would disconnect every agent still using it
pipeline-template (reusable YAML configuration) list; a configuration-management surface, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, no consuming stream this pass
single pipeline-template detail; part of the excluded templates surface
creates a pipeline template; part of the excluded templates surface
updates a pipeline template; part of the excluded templates surface
deletes a pipeline template; part of the excluded templates surface, would also detach it from every pipeline still referencing it
organization-level access-control rule list (cross-pipeline/cross-cluster permission policies); a security-policy configuration surface, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single rule detail; part of the excluded rules surface
creates an access-control rule; a security-policy mutation, out of scope for this data-sync connector
deletes an access-control rule; a security-policy mutation, out of scope for this data-sync connector
Buildkite Portals (scoped, shareable build-trigger links) list; a niche access-delegation feature, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-portal detail; part of the excluded portals surface
creates a portal (a scoped external trigger credential); credential-adjacent provisioning, out of scope
updates a portal; part of the excluded portals surface
deletes a portal; part of the excluded portals surface
organization audit-log event stream; a high-volume security/compliance event log, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, no consuming stream this pass
single audit-event detail; part of the excluded audit-log surface
organization-membership list (every user with account access, independent of team); Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, no consuming stream this pass
single organization-member detail; part of the excluded org-members surface
returns the API's own published webhook-IP-allowlist metadata, not organization/pipeline data
returns the set of emoji shortcuts usable in pipeline annotations/build messages; a UI-rendering asset list, not syncable operational data
introspects the CALLING token's own scopes/uuid, not a syncable object stream
revokes the token this connector is authenticating with; irreversibly ends the connector's own session, never a data operation
returns the identity of the currently-authenticated user, not a syncable object stream
